description:
Add a repo-level "target_branch" field to .balls/config.json. When set, bl review uses it instead of git_current_branch(&store.root) when deciding the integration target. Threads through:
- src/review.rs:94 (merge-in step) and the subsequent squash-merge.
- src/delivery.rs:32 (tag-scan branch).
- src/commands/sync.rs (sync of integration branch alongside state branch).

Default unset → falls back to current behavior (current branch at repo root). Document this fallback explicitly — today it is implicit and fragile.

WHY: today's "use whatever HEAD points to at the repo root" is undocumented and fragile (a stray git checkout main re-targets every subsequent review). Making the integration target explicit is also a strict precondition for deferred-squash mode: the forge plugin needs to know which branch the PR targets.

ACCEPTANCE:
- New repo with target_branch=null (or absent): all existing tests pass unchanged.
- target_branch=develop on a repo with main checked out at root: bl review squashes into develop, not main.
- bl sync still pushes the configured target_branch alongside the state branch.
- delivery.rs tag-scan uses target_branch when resolving delivered_in hints.
- Unit tests for the config knob and integration tests for the review path against a non-default branch.
title: target_branch config: make integration branch explicit
